title: Your first string
instruction:
A string is any text — names, messages, addresses, anything. You wrap it in double quotes.

Create a variable called favoriteFood and give it the name of your favorite food as a string. Then print it.
hint: favoriteFood := "Pizza"  — your text goes inside double quotes. Then fmt.Println(favoriteFood).

codeChecks.message: Create a string variable: favoriteFood := "Pizza" (use your real favorite food)
Print it: fmt.Println(favoriteFood)
Replace the TODO lines with your actual code.

title: Your first int
instruction:
An int is a whole number — no decimal point, no quotes. Ages, scores, years, counts. All ints.

Create a variable called age with your age as a whole number. Then print it.

Remember: no quotes around the number.
hint: age := 24  — just the number, no quotes, no decimal. Then fmt.Println(age).

codeChecks.message: Create an int variable: age := 24 (use your real age, no quotes)
Print it: fmt.Println(age)
Replace the TODO lines with your actual code.

title: Your first float64
instruction:
When you need a decimal point, you use float64. Prices, temperatures, percentages.

Create a variable called price with any price you choose — something with a decimal like 4.99 or 12.50. Then print it.
hint: price := 4.99  — the decimal point is what makes it a float64. No quotes. Then fmt.Println(price).

codeChecks.message: Create a float64 variable: price := 4.99 (must have a decimal point)
Print it: fmt.Println(price)
Replace the TODO lines with your actual code.

title: Your first bool
instruction:
A bool holds exactly one of two values: true or false. That's all it can ever be.

Create a variable called isWeekend. Set it to true or false depending on whether today is actually a weekend for you. Then print it.

Important: do NOT put true or false in quotes. They are not strings.
hint: isWeekend := true  or  isWeekend := false  — no quotes. Then fmt.Println(isWeekend).

codeChecks.message: Create a bool variable: isWeekend := true  (or false — no quotes!)
Print it: fmt.Println(isWeekend)
Replace the TODO lines with your actual code.

title: All four types — your complete profile
instruction:
You have learned all four basic data types. Let's use them all together.

Create four variables:
- name (string) — your name
- age (int) — your age as a whole number
- height (float64) — your height in meters like 1.75
- isStudent (bool) — true if you are a student, false if not

Then print all four.
hint: Four := lines — one for each type. name gets quotes, age gets a whole number, height gets a decimal, isStudent gets true or false (no quotes). Then four Println calls.

codeChecks.message: Create name as a string: name := "YourName"
Create age as an int: age := 24
Create height as a float64: height := 1.75 (must have a decimal)
Create isStudent as a bool: isStudent := true  (or false, no quotes)
Print name: fmt.Println(name)
Print age: fmt.Println(age)
Print height: fmt.Println(height)
Print isStudent: fmt.Println(isStudent)
Replace the TODO lines with your actual code.
